roles.title: Principal Product Manager, Agentic Commerce
roles.rationale: Principal PM, 0-to-1 agentic commerce with LLM agents, consumer + B2B2C retail. Strong domain, AI signal, and scale match.
roles.concerns: Requires 2+ years LLM production experience; Vikram's AI work is recent (N28, Omatochi). Verify depth of generative AI shipping.

roles.title: Vice President, Product
roles.rationale: VP Product at marketplace (eCommerce, buyer/seller, trust/safety). Deep AI/LLM requirement, strong domain match, enterprise scale. Perfect title and scope fit.

roles.title: Staff Product Manager, Enterprise (Industries)
roles.location: San Francisco, Seattle, New York, Chicago, Atlanta, Remote
roles.rationale: Staff PM, Enterprise at Stripe—payments infrastructure, regulated verticals (insurance, healthcare, public sector), 0-to-1 shipping, API design. Direct domain and compliance overlap with VF Adyen, Wells Fargo KYC, Metavante work.
roles.concerns: IC-track role (no direct reports), not management path; smaller scope than Head of Product tenure at N28, but enterprise/platform scope compensates.

roles.title: Staff Product Manager, Payments
roles.rationale: Staff PM at Stripe PayIntel, payments infrastructure, checkout, multi-stakeholder alignment. Direct domain match with VF Adyen fraud/compliance work; senior IC title.
roles.concerns: Staff PM is IC-track, not management; Vikram's trajectory favors management (Head of Product, Director). Score reflects strong domain/scale fit but title path divergence.

roles.title: Director Product Management, Banking + AP
roles.location: New York, New York, United States
roles.rationale: Director role at scale: $10B+ deposits, $1B monthly AP volume, ACH/wires/RTP payments, regulated banking, team leadership. Direct overlap with Metavante/Wells Fargo/VF payments modernization expertise.
roles.concerns: NYC office requirement (3 days/week) vs. Denver base; AI positioning secondary to banking infrastructure focus.

roles.title: Director Product Management, Banking + AP
roles.rationale: Director-level FinTech role: banking, payments (ACH, wires, RTP), regulated compliance, team leadership. Direct overlap with Wells Fargo, Metavante, VF payments modernization.
roles.concerns: Location requires SF/Seattle/NYC 3 days/week; Vikram is Denver-based. AI signal present but not core to the role.

roles.title: Director Product Management, Banking + AP
roles.rationale: Director, Banking + AP at Brex. $10B+ deposits, $1B monthly AP volume, regulated systems (ACH, wires, RTP), team leadership. Direct overlap with Metavante/Wells Fargo and VF payments modernization experience.
roles.concerns: Seattle location requires 3 days/week onsite; Vikram is Denver-based but open to US travel. AI mention is aspirational ('as we incorporate agents') not core to current role scope.

roles.title: Group Product Manager
roles.location: New York, New York, United States
roles.rationale: Group Product Manager at Brex—owns fintech platform, spend management, AI-native automation, multi-team leadership. Direct domain and title match with strong AI signal.
roles.concerns: NYC office requirement (3 days/week) vs. Denver base, though relocation flexibility stated in profile mitigates.

roles.title: Group Product Manager
roles.rationale: Group PM at fintech leader; owns multi-team portfolio, AI/ML integration, regulated environment. Strong title, domain, and AI signal match.
roles.concerns: Office requirement (3 days/week SF/Seattle/NYC) conflicts with Denver base, though candidate open to travel.

roles.title: Group Product Manager
roles.rationale: Group PM at Brex (fintech, B2B SaaS, payments). Owns strategy, manages PMs, AI/automation core. Direct overlap with VF Adyen modernization and Metavante work.
roles.concerns: Seattle office requirement (3 days/week) vs. Denver base, though relocation flexibility stated in profile.

roles.title: Staff Product Manager
roles.location: New York, New York, United States
roles.rationale: Staff PM at Brex, fintech infrastructure with AI-native automation, multi-quarter strategy, cross-functional leadership. Direct domain and seniority match.
roles.concerns: NYC office 3 days/week may conflict with Denver base; Staff PM is IC-track, not management path Vikram typically pursues.

roles.title: Senior Director, Product Management
roles.rationale: Senior Director, FinTech/payments, owns fraud, credit decisioning, pricing—direct overlap with VF Adyen and Metavante experience. Team leadership, regulated environment.

roles.title: Group Product Manager, Fraud
roles.rationale: Group PM, fraud decisioning and authentication. Direct overlap with VF Adyen fraud work ($24M loss reduction). Strong AI signal, PCI-DSS, payments expertise, remote Denver-compatible.

roles.title: Director of Product Management, Commercial Banking
roles.rationale: Director, Commercial Banking at regulated bank; 0→1 Wires/ACH/FedNow platform ownership. Direct fintech+payments+compliance overlap with VF Adyen and Metavante ACH experience.
roles.concerns: No AI signal in JD; B2B/commercial banking differs from Vikram's B2C retail checkout focus, though payments infra transfers cleanly.

roles.title: Principal Product Manager - Fraud, Risk & ML Platform
roles.rationale: Principal PM, fraud/risk ML platform at regulated fintech bank. Direct overlap: fraud decisioning, ML ops, compliance, API infrastructure. Strong technical scope.
roles.concerns: Platform/infrastructure IC role, not people management; Vikram's recent trajectory favors leadership scope, but Principal is legitimate senior track match.

roles.title: Principal Product Manager, Real-Time Payments Platform
roles.rationale: Principal PM, real-time payments platform at regulated bank. Deep payments rail expertise (FedNow, Fedwire, RTP, ACH), compliance (AML/KYC/OFAC), vendor management, platform infrastructure. Direct Metavante/VF Adyen overlap.
roles.concerns: No explicit AI/ML signal in payments orchestration, fraud decisioning, or decisioning infrastructure—role focuses on rails and compliance infrastructure rather than applied AI.

roles.title: Staff Product Manager, FinTech & Ops
roles.location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
roles.rationale: Staff PM, FinTech & Ops at Faire: payments routing, underwriting decisioning, ML integrations, compliance. Strong domain and title fit; IC track vs. management.
roles.concerns: Staff PM is IC-track; Vikram's trajectory is management-heavy (Head of Product, Director). Role lacks people leadership scope.

roles.title: Senior Director, Product Management (Digital)
roles.rationale: Senior Director, eComm at premium retail brand. Direct match: conversion, AOV, product discovery, checkout confidence. 11+ years VF retail experience maps precisely.
roles.concerns: NYC onsite requirement (Denver-based); no AI signal; smaller brand scale than $100M+ GMV history.

roles.title: Director of Product, Growth/AI
roles.rationale: Director of Product, Growth/AI at FinTech leader. Strong AI signal (agents, ML models, account scoring). FinTech domain, regulated environment, GTM systems, team management align with VF payments modernization and leadership experience.
roles.concerns: Growth/GTM focus secondary to Vikram's core payments and commerce expertise; Seattle office requirement (not Denver), though remote flexibility partial offset.

roles.title: Director of Product, Growth/AI
roles.rationale: Director title, FinTech (corporate spend/payments), AI-native platform, GTM systems, team management. Strong domain and AI signal; location constraint (3 days/week SF/NYC/Seattle) vs. Denver base is minor friction.
roles.concerns: Location requires 3 days/week onsite; Vikram is Denver-based. Growth/GTM focus secondary to core payments/fraud expertise, but adjacent and well-scoped.

roles.title: Director of Product, Growth/AI
roles.location: New York, New York, United States
roles.rationale: Director-level, FinTech/payments platform (Brex), AI agents for GTM, account scoring ML, regulated environment. Strong title and domain fit; GTM focus slightly adjacent to Vikram's core P&L ownership.
roles.concerns: Growth/GTM specialization less central than checkout/payments/fraud core; NYC office 3 days/week vs. remote preference.

roles.title: Staff Product Manager
roles.rationale: Staff PM at Brex: fintech, payments infrastructure, AI-native automation, regulated environment. Strong domain + title fit; location (SF/NYC/Seattle) requires relocation from Denver.
roles.concerns: 3-day/week office requirement in SF/NYC/Seattle conflicts with Denver base; candidate prefers full remote/flexible.

roles.title: Staff Product Manager
roles.rationale: Staff PM at Brex—fintech platform, financial infrastructure, spend management, payments ecosystem. Strong domain and title fit; AI-native mentioned but underspecified.
roles.concerns: Staff IC role, not management track. Seattle office 3 days/week may conflict with remote preference, though acceptable.

roles.title: Staff Product Manager, Capital
roles.rationale: Staff PM, Capital Risk & Reporting at FinTech unicorn. 7+ years required, regulated environment, deep financial logic, cross-functional leadership. Strong domain and seniority match.
roles.concerns: No explicit AI signal despite data/analytics focus. Role is infrastructure/systems-heavy, not consumer-facing product.

roles.title: Group Product Manager, Invest
roles.location: CA - San Francisco; NY - New York City
roles.rationale: Group Product Manager at regulated FinTech bank (SoFi); brokerage/investing domain, team leadership, compliance-heavy environment. Strong title and domain match; smaller scope than VF Global Director.
roles.concerns: Investing/brokerage is adjacent to Vikram's payments expertise. No explicit AI signal in JD. Location (SF/NYC) acceptable but not Denver-based.

roles.title: Principal Product Manager, AI Features
roles.rationale: Principal PM, AI-driven financial guidance at regulated bank. LLM/personalization, compliance environment, and strategic scope align strongly with Vikram's FinTech and AI positioning.
roles.concerns: IC track (Principal) rather than management; San Francisco location requires relocation from Denver, though remote flexibility not explicitly stated.

roles.title: Principal Product Manager, Member 360
roles.rationale: Principal PM, Member 360 data platform at regulated FinTech bank. Strong domain fit (fintech, payments, fraud/AML), compliance signals (regulatory, governed data), AI orchestration. Enterprise scale, multi-org leadership.
roles.concerns: Data platform PM is adjacent but not core to Vikram's checkout/payments/fraud decisioning strength; data governance/lineage less directly tied to revenue impact work.

roles.title: Principal Product Manager, Membership
roles.rationale: Principal PM at regulated FinTech bank (SoFi) owning membership/loyalty strategy, multi-product ecosystem, compliance. Strong title, domain, and scale alignment with VF payments modernization and loyalty experience.
roles.concerns: Role is IC-track Principal, not management of other PMs. No explicit AI signal in membership/rewards scope.

roles.title: Principal Product Manager, Originations Core
roles.rationale: Principal PM, lending originations and credit decisioning at regulated FinTech national bank. Strong domain, title, and systems thinking match with Vikram's VF/Wells/Metavante payments and fraud background.
roles.concerns: Credit decisioning mentioned but no explicit AI/ML signal; role is IC-track principal, not management—slightly below Head of Product trajectory.

roles.title: Staff Product Manager, Cortex
roles.location: Menlo Park, CA; New York, NY
roles.rationale: Staff PM, AI-native FinTech product (LLM, RAG, agentic systems, evaluation frameworks). Strong AI + regulated finance domain match; senior IC role aligns with Vikram's expertise.
roles.concerns: 3-day/week in-office requirement (Menlo Park or NYC) vs. Denver base; no management scope listed, pure IC track.

roles.title: Director, Product Management, MyPay
roles.location: New York, NY, USA; San Francisco, CA, USA
roles.rationale: Director title, FinTech (lending/payroll), team leadership, regulated consumer product. Strong domain and seniority match; compensation aligns.
roles.concerns: MyPay is early-stage product (not mature scale like VF checkout); no explicit AI/fraud signals in JD despite FinTech context.
